name: Basílica de Nuestra Señora de la Peña y Ermita de San Pedro - circular trail running from Graus
name_original: Basílica de Nuestra Señora de la Peña y Ermita de San Pedro – circular de trail running desde Graus
region_intro: Jogging routes around Graus are set within the Spanish Pre-Pyrenees, characterized by a diverse landscape of riverine paths along the Ésera and Isábena rivers and rolling foothills. The region's varied topography offers both relatively flat terrain alongside waterways and more challenging ascents into the surrounding hills. Extensive forested areas and natural features provide a mix of natural surfaces for running. This environment supports a range of running experiences, from easier jogs to more demanding trail running. Best jogging routes around Graus The most popular jogging route is Basílica de Nuestra Señora de la Peña y Ermita de San Pedro - circular trail running from Graus, a 2.7 miles (4.4 km) trail that takes about 35 minutes to complete. This moderate route features an elevation gain of nearly 200 meters. Another top favourite among local runners is Mirador del Morral – Chapel of San Rafael de Kanna loop from Graus, a difficult 8.5 miles (13.6 km) path. This route offers significant elevation changes, climbing over 540 meters through varied terrain. Local runners also love the Graus – Puente de Abajo loop from Graus, a 6.6 miles (10.6 km) trail leading through riverine landscapes and local countryside, often completed in about 1 hour 20 minutes. Jogging around Graus is defined by riverine paths, rolling foothills, and forested areas. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate jogs to difficult trail runs. The routes in Graus are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 1 review. More than 160 runners have used komoot to explore Graus's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of terrain can I expect on running routes around Graus?
A: Running routes around Graus offer a diverse range of terrain. You'll find relatively flat riverine paths along the Ésera and Isábena rivers, ideal for easier jogs. For more challenging runs, the Pyrenean foothills provide rolling hills and ascents with natural surfaces like grass and dirt, perfect for trail running.

Q: Are there running routes suitable for beginners in Graus?
A: While there are no routes specifically classified as 'easy' in our current selection, many of the moderate routes can be enjoyed by beginners looking for a gentle challenge. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e950305733/basilica-de-nuestra-senora-de-la-pena-y-ermita-de-san-pedro-circular-trail-running-from-graus" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Basílica de Nuestra Señora de la Peña y Ermita de San Pedro - circular trail running from Graus&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 4.4 km route with a manageable elevation gain, offering a good introduction to the area's trails.

Q: How many running routes are available in the Graus area?
A: There are 13 running routes available around Graus, offering a variety of distances and difficulty levels to suit different preferences and fitness levels.

Q: What do other runners think about the trails in Graus?
A: The running routes in Graus are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from 1 review. Over 160 runners have used komoot to explore Graus's varied terrain, often praising the scenic riverine paths and the challenging yet rewarding ascents into the foothills.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Graus?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Graus are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31814060" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Graus – Puente de Abajo loop from Graus&lt;/a&gt;, a 10.6 km trail that takes you through riverine landscapes and local countryside.

Q: What natural features or viewpoints can I see while running near Graus?
A: The region offers stunning natural features. You can enjoy views of the Barasona Reservoir, or the Torreciudad Sanctuary and the Grado I reservoir. These areas provide expansive water views and picturesque backdrops for your run. For specific viewpoints, consider routes that pass by highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4990014" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Barasona Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or attractions along the running routes?
A: Yes, the area around Graus is rich in history. While running, you might encounter historical sites such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4446036" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Samitier Castle&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4446024" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Saints Emeterius and Celedonius&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e950305733/basilica-de-nuestra-senora-de-la-pena-y-ermita-de-san-pedro-circular-trail-running-from-graus" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Basílica de Nuestra Señora de la Peña y Ermita de San Pedro - circular trail running from Graus&lt;/a&gt; route itself passes by significant religious buildings.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Graus?
A: Graus, situated in the Spanish Pyrenees, offers pleasant conditions for running during spring and autumn when temperatures are mild. Summer can be warm, especially at lower elevations, so early morning or late evening runs are recommended. Winter can bring colder temperatures, particularly at higher altitudes, but many lower-elevation riverine paths remain accessible.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging running trails in Graus?
A: For experienced trail runners seeking a challenge, Graus offers several difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10465966" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador del Morral – Chapel of San Rafael de Kanna loop from Graus&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 13.6 km path with over 540 meters of elevation gain, providing a significant workout and stunning views.

Q: Can I find running routes that offer varied elevation changes?
A: Absolutely. The Pre-Pyrenees region around Graus is known for its varied topography. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40010627" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Graus – Chapel of San Rafael de Kanna loop from Graus&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 10.5 km route with over 340 meters of elevation gain, provide excellent opportunities for runners looking to incorporate hills into their training.

Q: Are there any running routes that stay relatively flat?
A: Yes, if you prefer less elevation, routes that follow the riverine paths along the Ésera and Isábena rivers tend to be flatter. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38158053" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de Abajo loop from Graus&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 5.7 km route with minimal elevation gain, making it a good option for a more consistent, flatter jog.
tips: The Basilica of the Virgen de la Peña is, without a doubt, the most emblematic building in the city of Graus. As its name indicates, the basilica stands on a hill guarded by a great rock wall. The current temple was built in the 16th century, although there was already a Romanesque sanctuary before. It is well worth visiting the cloister of the basilica, from where you have fantastic views of Graus and the surroundings. In summer, classical music concerts are also held in the cloister.
There is a via ferrata that passes under this viewpoint, the Peña del Morral via ferrata.
Good views of the Ésera and Isábena valleys from here!
